front: What is the primary purpose of the OSI model?
back: To provide a standard framework for designing and understanding network communication by dividing the process into seven distinct, logical layers.

front: What are the four layers of the TCP/IP protocol suite?
back: 1. Link Layer (Network Interface), 2. Internet Layer (IP), 3. Transport Layer (TCP, UDP), 4. Application Layer.

front: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for logical addressing and routing?
What is the key difference between TCP and UDP at the Transport Layer?
back: TCP is connection-oriented, reliable, and ensures delivery with error checking. UDP is connectionless, unreliable, and offers faster transmission with no delivery guarantees.

front: Define the role of the Data Link Layer (Layer 2) in the OSI model.
back: It is responsible for node-to-node delivery, framing, physical addressing (MAC addresses), error control, flow control, and access control to the transmission medium.
